response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack/web engineer, but the match to this role is poor on nearly every required qualification. The position requires native-level Japanese, which the resume does not list (English, Romanian, German only). Strong C for UNIX and Linux kernel source-modification experience are required, yet the candidate's C/C++ work is limited to a brief 2014-2015 IDE-tooling role, with a career otherwise centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang web platforms. There is no evidence of L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X, and no embedded firmware experience beyond passing mentions.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some low-level work
Some C/C++ background (NI LabWindows/CVI) and long-standing Linux familiarity
Strong engineering-practice culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, while native-level Japanese is a hard requirement
No demonstrated Linux kernel, L2/L3 networking, switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) experience
C for UNIX experience is brief and dated; recent career is entirely web/full-stack rather than embedded firmware
